question_text: Why is it easier to tighten a bolt with a longer spanner than a shorter one?

options.label: B
options.text: You can apply the same force at a greater distance from the pivot

options.label: C
options.text: The longer spanner reduces the size of the bolt

correct_answer: You can apply the same force at a greater distance from the pivot
explanation: A longer spanner has a greater perpendicular distance from the pivot point (the bolt centre). Since moment (turning effect) = force × perpendicular distance, applying the same force at a greater distance creates a larger moment, making it easier to turn the bolt.
